seo.title: Compress PDF and keep it readable - Browser guide
seo.description: Reduce PDF size in your browser while checking readability, page count, and practical limits instead of relying on a blind compression promise.

h1: Compress PDF while keeping it readable
lead: Shrink everyday PDFs with a review step so the result stays useful for forms, study material, and attachments.

strings.breadcrumbCurrent: Compress PDF and keep it readable
strings.ctaText: Compression is only useful if the final PDF can still be read. Use the tool, then inspect the result before you send it.
strings.toolCardText: Try structural cleanup first and download the result for a page-by-page check.
strings.stepsLead: Treat compression as a draft result, not an automatic finish line.
strings.stepOneText: Use the original PDF when possible instead of a re-saved screenshot or phone scan.
strings.stepTwoText: Run the browser tool and avoid changing the source file until you know the output is acceptable.
strings.stepThreeText: Zoom in on small text, signatures, tables, and stamped areas before sharing.
strings.limitationsTitle: Readability depends on the original PDF
strings.limitationsText: A text-based PDF usually survives compression better than a low-resolution scan. Some files need source-level changes instead.
strings.limitationsItemOne: If the original scan is blurry, compression cannot restore detail.
strings.limitationsItemTwo: If images dominate the document, size reduction may trade off against clarity.
strings.limitationsItemThree: If a platform has a strict size cap, you may need to remove pages or export from the source again.
strings.privacyText: The file contents are read by the browser for the tool action. Normal site assets, analytics, and advertising requests can still load.
strings.privacyItemOneText: The tool is designed for quick one-off document cleanup without a login.
strings.privacyItemTwoText: If the PDF contains regulated or confidential content, check whether browser tools are allowed in your workflow.
strings.relatedLead: Readable output can depend on page order, extra pages, and target requirements.
strings.relatedCompressText: Reduce size while keeping review steps visible.
strings.relatedUploadText: Compare file size and preview the result before sending.
strings.relatedManageText: Remove pages that do not need to be included.
strings.faqOneAnswer: Some structural cleanup can reduce size without obvious visual change, but every result should be checked because PDFs vary.
strings.faqTwoQuestion: Which PDFs usually keep readability best?
strings.faqTwoAnswer: Text-based PDFs and documents exported from the original source usually behave better than photo-heavy scans.
strings.faqThreeQuestion: What if the result looks worse?
strings.faqThreeAnswer: Use the original file, reduce image size in the source document, or remove unnecessary pages before trying again.
